verdict_reasoning: SRC is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation — canonical localization is cytoplasmic-facing. However, SRC has documented cell-surface (ecto-kinase) display on tumor cells and activated platelets, where an outer-leaflet pool has been detected and implicated in extracellular substrate phosphorylation. This cancer/activation-state ecto-display qualifies as contextual. Not yes (baseline is inner leaflet); not no (documented non-baseline ecto-pool). No lysosomal exocytosis or wash-resistant TM tethering; no germline-restricted display; no covalent TM-partner anchoring.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Strength/reproducibility of SRC ecto-kinase surface display evidence is limited; baseline biology is overwhelmingly inner-leaflet cytoplasmic.
